title: Quizzer AI
description: An AI-powered adaptive learning platform that generates unlimited, tailored programming quizzes using Google Gemini. Features a 3-step generative pipeline, context-aware explanations, and a gamified progression system built on a Service-Oriented Django architecture.
longDescription:
Quizzer AI replaces static question banks with dynamically generated programming quizzes powered by the Google Gemini API. A custom 3-step AI pipeline handles intent parsing from natural language prompts, structured quiz generation with strict JSON enforcement, and targeted mistake analysis that provides context-aware explanations for incorrect answers.

Built on a Service-Oriented Django backend with HTMX and Alpine.js on the frontend, the platform delivers SPA-like interactivity without a separate JavaScript framework. It includes a full gamification system with XP progression, leveling, daily streaks, and 9 unlockable achievement badges. Authentication is handled through a custom user model with email as the primary identifier, complete with email verification, password recovery, and CSP header enforcement in production. Code snippets are rendered with Prism.js syntax highlighting, and the interface supports both dark and light themes with persistent user preferences.
features: Context-Aware Mistake Analysis and Guided Explanations
Service-Oriented Django Architecture with Modular Apps
Email Verification, Password Recovery & CSP Enforcement
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/quizzer-ai.git
cd quizzer-ai

# Setup environment
cp .env.example .env
# Add your GEMINI_API_KEY to .env

# Enter project folder and install dependencies
cd qtrmrs
uv sync

# Run migrations and start server
uv run python manage.py migrate
uv run python manage.py runserver

title: Algorithm Visualizer
description: An interactive educational platform that executes sorting and pathfinding algorithms on a Python FastAPI backend and streams execution states in real-time to a React frontend via WebSockets.
longDescription:
AlgoVisualizer runs algorithm code on an asynchronous Python backend using generator-based execution, streaming each step to the React frontend over persistent WebSocket connections. This approach avoids pre-calculated browser-side animations and enables true real-time visualization with accurate state representation.

The platform supports Bubble Sort, Insertion Sort, and Selection Sort alongside Dijkstra, BFS, and DFS pathfinding algorithms. Pathfinding operates in a dual-mode hybrid view, allowing users to toggle between 2D grid layouts and physics-based node-link graph layouts with semantic zoom. A VCR-style playback engine provides play, pause, step-through, and adjustable speed controls (10ms to 500ms), with synchronized pseudocode line highlighting and an auto-scrolling execution log.
features: Sorting & Pathfinding with Interactive Grids and Graphs
Dual-Mode View: 2D Grid and Physics-Based Node-Link Graph
Dynamic Maze Generation & Custom Dataset Input
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/algorithm-visualizer.git
cd algorithm-visualizer

# Install dependencies
cd backend && uv sync && cd ..
cd frontend && npm install && cd ..

# Terminal 1: Backend
cd backend && uv run uvicorn app.main:api --reload

# Terminal 2: Frontend
cd frontend && npm run dev

title: GitNexus
description: A self-hosted GitHub companion dashboard for developers. Browse user profiles and bulk-download repositories, track release assets across watched repos, and time-travel through commit history with isolated dev servers powered by Git worktrees.
longDescription:
GitNexus is a local-first developer tool built with a decoupled React SPA frontend and an async Python FastAPI backend, backed by SQLite with Fernet symmetric encryption for securely persisting GitHub API tokens and scoped environment variables on the local machine.

The Repo Replay module clones repositories, syncs their full commit history, and uses Git worktrees to browse the codebase at any point in time. Scoped environment variables (global, project, or commit-level) can be attached before spawning isolated HTTP dev servers for any commit. The Discovery Engine fetches GitHub user profiles, renders contribution graphs, and supports multi-repo bulk downloads. The Asset Watchtower monitors tracked repositories for new releases and enables direct asset downloads.
features: Repo Replay: Browse Commit History via Git Worktrees
Isolated HTTP Dev Servers per Commit State
Asset Watchtower: GitHub Release Tracking & Downloads
Discovery Engine: User Profiles & Bulk Repo Downloads
Fernet Symmetric Encryption for Tokens & Env Vars
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/git-nexus.git
cd git-nexus

# Install dependencies
cd backend && uv sync && cd ..
cd frontend && npm install && cd ..

# Terminal 1: Backend
cd backend && uv run uvicorn main:app --reload

# Terminal 2: Frontend
cd frontend && npm run dev

title: Arcade Nexus
description: A browser-based gaming console built with React and FastAPI, featuring an offline-first SQLite backend, a typed TypeScript Game SDK for 2D and 3D game development, local profiles, save states, achievements, and gamepad support.
longDescription:
Arcade Nexus is a web application designed to replicate a console-like gaming experience in the desktop browser. It uses a decoupled architecture with a React frontend managed by Zustand and a Python FastAPI backend backed by async SQLite via SQLAlchemy.

The platform operates entirely offline with local profiles for multi-user access. A typed TypeScript Game SDK provides a unified interface for building 2D Canvas and 3D Three.js games, handling game loops, keyboard and gamepad input, and state persistence. Games support multiple save slots, playtime and high score tracking, and a global achievement system. The interface uses a premium console aesthetic with charcoal and zinc tones, immersive boot sequences, and fullscreen gameplay.
features: Typed TypeScript Game SDK (2D Canvas & 3D Three.js)
Local Save States & Global Achievement System
Premium Console UI with Gamepad Support
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/arcade-nexus.git
cd arcade-nexus

# Install dependencies
cd backend && uv sync && cd ..
cd frontend && npm install && cd ..

# Terminal 1: Backend
cd backend && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Terminal 2: Frontend
cd frontend && npm run dev

title: Tremors Music
description: A local-first music player available as both a web application and a native Tauri desktop app. Features a Python FastAPI backend for metadata extraction and audio streaming, a React frontend with virtualized lists, and an Apple Music-inspired UI.
longDescription:
Tremors Music is a privacy-focused music player that runs as a web app or a native desktop application. The Python FastAPI backend handles ID3 metadata parsing via Mutagen, library scanning, and range-based audio streaming, while the React frontend provides virtualized lists for handling libraries of 10,000+ songs, synchronized LRC lyrics display, and smart playlists.

The desktop version uses Tauri with the Python backend compiled as a sidecar process, achieving a ~100-200MB RAM footprint compared to ~700-900MB in a browser tab. The application supports MP3, FLAC, M4A, WAV, OGG, WMA, AAC, and ALAC formats. All music data stays locally on the user's machine with no cloud dependencies.
features: Native Desktop App via Tauri with Python Sidecar
Synchronized LRC Lyrics & ID3 Metadata Extraction
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/tremors-music.git
cd tremors-music

# Install Dependencies
cd backend && uv sync && cd ..
cd frontend && npm install && cd ..

# Run Web Version
# Terminal 1: Backend
cd backend && uv run uvicorn main:app --reload
# Terminal 2: Frontend
cd frontend && npm run dev

# Run Desktop Version (Tauri)
cd tauri && npm install && npm run dev

title: Cosmos
description: A browser-based 3D space exploration simulator built with Three.js and React. Features a Solar System with 2K NASA textures, a secondary Quantumania dimension with 38 lazy-loaded 3D models, custom GLSL shaders, and 6-DOF flight controls with gamepad support.
longDescription:
Cosmos is a WebGL-based 3D universe exploration engine built with Three.js, React, and TypeScript. It features a multi-system architecture with a Solar System and a secondary Quantumania realm, navigated through 6-DOF flight controls with progressive boost mechanics and full gamepad support.

The Solar System contains 9 planets with 2K NASA textures, 4 moons, 2,000 instanced asteroid meshes, and a GLSL raymarched black hole. A radar minimap enables teleportation to the Quantumania dimension, which contains 38 lazy-loaded GLB models including floating mountains, structures, and the Arishem celestial entity with interior multi-point lighting. Custom GLSL shaders handle sun granulation, Earth day/night cycles, and atmospheric glow effects. A centralized physics SDK manages orbital mechanics with Kepler-based calculations.
features: 6-DOF Flight Controls with Keyboard & Gamepad Support
Custom GLSL Shaders (Sun, Earth Cycle, Raymarched Black Hole)
Radar Minimap with Entity Tracking & Teleportation
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/cosmos.git
cd cosmos/cosmos-app

# Install dependencies and run
npm install
npm run dev

title: LocalGhost
description: A local-first security auditing and benchmarking toolkit for development servers. Runs 11 asynchronous scanners covering port discovery, security headers, SSL/TLS analysis, CORS auditing, load testing, and DDoS resilience with weighted scoring and report exports.
longDescription:
LocalGhost is a Python-based security auditing CLI tool built with FastAPI, designed for hardening local development environments. It executes 11 concurrent asynchronous scanners: port scanning with service identification across 35+ known services, vulnerability detection for 7 security headers and 18 sensitive file paths, SSL/TLS certificate and cipher suite analysis, CORS misconfiguration detection, cookie security auditing, technology fingerprinting, DNS enumeration, and XSS scanning.

Beyond security auditing, it performs configurable load testing with percentile latency metrics (p50/p95/p99) and DDoS resilience testing including connection flood and slowloris scenarios. Results are scored on a weighted 0-100 scale with letter grades (A+ through F) and category breakdowns. All scans are persisted to a local SQLite database for historical comparison, with JSON report export capability. The frontend uses a Material Design 3 dashboard built with Vanilla HTML, CSS, and ES Modules.
features: Weighted 0-100 Scoring Engine with Letter Grades
Configurable Load Testing & DDoS Resilience Testing
SQLite Scan History with JSON Report Export
XSS Detection, CORS Audit & SSL/TLS Analysis
installation:
# Clone and navigate
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/localghost.git
cd localghost/localghost

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Run the project
uv run python -m backend.main

title: Earnslate
description: A client-side finance tracking platform built with Next.js and TypeScript. Manage transactions, set budgets with auto-reset, and track subscriptions with treemap visualizations all persisted locally via browser storage.
longDescription:
Earnslate is a personal finance management application built entirely on Next.js (App Router) with TypeScript and CSS Modules. All data is persisted in the browser via Zustand with localStorage, ensuring financial data never leaves the user's device.

The dashboard renders real-time spending breakdowns with interactive pie and donut charts. A transaction ledger supports search, multi-variable filtering, date presets, pagination, and batch deletion. The budgeting system tracks progress per category and automatically resets on period expiry. A subscription manager includes 100+ pre-loaded service templates and uses treemap visualizations to show recurring cost distribution. Data can be exported and imported via CSV/JSON for backup and portability.
features: Client-Side Next.js App with Zustand State
Monochrome UI with Dark/Light Mode Toggle
Budget Tracking with Automated Period Expiry
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/earnslate.git
cd earnslate/earnslate-app

# Install dependencies and run
npm install
npm run dev

title: Xcrape
description: An asynchronous Python web scraper powered by Playwright and BeautifulSoup4. Extracts structured data, detects 15+ technologies, captures screenshots, and archives results to SQLite all through a Material Design 3 terminal-themed UI.
longDescription:
Xcrape is a locally-hosted web scraping tool built with FastAPI that uses headless Chromium via Playwright for JavaScript-rendered page extraction. It parses pages using BeautifulSoup4 to extract meta tags, headings, links, tables, images, JSON-LD, OpenGraph metadata, and social media profiles. A technology detection module fingerprints 15+ frameworks and CMS platforms.

Each scrape job captures a JPEG screenshot and archives all extracted data to a local SQLite database via aiosqlite. Users can filter historical jobs, trigger one-click re-scrapes, download scraped images individually or as bulk ZIP archives, and export results in CSV or JSON formats. The interface uses a terminal-themed Material Design 3 aesthetic with JetBrains Mono typography.
features: Material Design 3 Terminal UI with JetBrains Mono
JPEG Screenshot Capture & Bulk Image Downloads
SQLite Archival with CSV/JSON Report Export
installation:
# Clone and navigate
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/xcrape.git
cd xcrape/xcrape

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Install Playwright browsers
uv run playwright install chromium

# Run the project
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

title: Amanzon E-Commerce
description: A full-stack e-commerce platform built with Django and PostgreSQL (Supabase). Handles product catalogs, Razorpay payments, order lifecycle management, discount coupons, user reviews, and email-verified authentication.
longDescription:
Amanzon is a full-featured e-commerce application built on Django with a Supabase PostgreSQL database. It uses server-side rendering with Bootstrap for a responsive storefront interface with dark mode support.

The platform implements the complete purchase lifecycle: product browsing with category filtering and search, persistent cart and wishlist, multi-address management, discount coupon validation, and checkout via the Razorpay API with a demo mode for testing. Order management supports status tracking, cancellation, and refund processing. Authentication uses email-verified registration with OTP-based password recovery and rate limiting. Administrative features include product management, coupon creation, and order oversight through the Django admin panel.
features: Discount Coupon System with Usage Tracking
Email-Verified Auth with OTP Password Reset
Product Reviews, Multi-Address Management & Dark Mode
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/amanzon
cd amanzon/app

# Setup with uv
uv sync
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your credentials

# Initialize Database
uv run python manage.py migrate
uv run python manage.py createsuperuser

# Run Server
uv run python manage.py runserver

title: Social Network Platform
description: A frontend prototype of a social networking platform built with React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand. Features public feeds, direct messaging, community channels, user profiles, and a dual-mode navigation sidebar.
longDescription:
SocNet is a frontend prototype that combines public broadcast feeds and community-based channel spaces within a unified interface. Users can compose posts, interact through likes and replies, engage in direct and group messaging, and discover trending topics and communities.

Built with React, TypeScript, Vite, and Tailwind CSS, the application uses Zustand for client-side state management with simulated mock data. A dual-mode navigation sidebar contextually switches between global navigation and community channel lists depending on the active route. Community spaces support text and voice channels. The interface includes user profiles, notification center, settings panel, and an explore page.
features: Public Feed with Posts, Likes, Replies & Reposts
Community Spaces with Text & Voice Channels
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/social-network.git
cd social-network/frontend

# Install dependencies and run
npm install
npm run dev

title: Material Design
description: A zero-dependency implementation of Material Design 3, Material You, and Material 3 Expressive specifications in pure HTML, CSS custom properties, and vanilla JavaScript.
longDescription:
This project recreates Google's Material Design 3 component system entirely with vanilla web technologies and no build tools or framework dependencies. It uses CSS custom properties for theming, with a dynamic color engine supporting light/dark modes and multiple color seed palettes.

The implementation includes Buttons, Cards, Input fields, Dialogs, Bottom Sheets, Chips, and responsive Navigation components (Rail and Drawer), all injected dynamically via JavaScript. A custom ripple effect system replicates the material interaction pattern natively. An interactive playground page exposes all 300+ generated design tokens for real-time inspection and testing.
features: Zero Dependencies (No Build Tools or Frameworks)
Dynamic Light/Dark Mode & Color Seed Generation
MD3 Components (Buttons, Cards, Dialogs, Sheets, Chips)
Developer Playground with 300+ CSS Design Tokens
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/material-design.git
cd material-design

# Serve locally (requires a live server for modules/CORS)
# Option 1: Python
python -m http.server
# Option 2: Node
npx serve

title: ReConText
description: A statically generated knowledge base for developer cheat sheets and research notes. Built with Next.js and MDX, featuring auto-inferred metadata, interactive React components within markdown, and a dark "Cosmic Night" theme.
longDescription:
ReConText is a static knowledge management site built on Next.js with MDX content support via next-mdx-remote and rehype-pretty-code for syntax highlighting. Content files are dropped into a directory structure and processed with auto-inferred titles, dates, difficulty levels, and reading time estimates.

The platform supports MDX, allowing embedded React components such as tabbed code blocks and alert callouts within standard markdown documents. Navigation is handled through auto-generated breadcrumb trails and dynamically rendered Tables of Contents. The interface uses a "Cosmic Night" dark theme with glassmorphism accents and Framer Motion page transitions. The site is fully responsive and statically generated for fast load times.
features: MDX Content with Embedded React Components
Auto-Inferred Metadata (Title, Date, Difficulty, Reading Time)
Static Site Generation via Next.js App Router
Cosmic Night Dark Theme with Framer Motion Transitions
Responsive Layout for Mobile, Tablet & Desktop
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/recontext.git
cd recontext/recontext

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Run in development mode
npm run dev

title: Diff Viewer
description: A GitHub-style side-by-side diff viewer built with Next.js. Compares text and files with character-level intra-line highlighting, synchronized dual-panel scrolling, and drag-and-drop file upload support.
longDescription:
Diff Viewer is a browser-based text comparison tool built on Next.js and React. It uses the jsdiff library (Myers' diff algorithm) to perform character-level intra-line diffing, highlighting exact insertions and deletions within modified lines rather than marking entire lines as changed.

The interface presents original and modified content in side-by-side panels with synchronized scrolling. Users can paste raw text directly or drag and drop files for comparison. The UI uses a high-contrast dark theme inspired by GitHub Desktop.
features: Drag-and-Drop File Upload & Raw Text Pasting
installation:
# Clone and navigate
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/diff.git
cd diff/web

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Run the project
npm run dev

title: GTRMRS
description: A unified suite of dependency-free, Git-aware Python CLI tools. Includes rtree (directory tree visualization), locr (lines-of-code counting with language breakdown), and gitmig (clean repository migration).
longDescription:
GTRMRS is a Python CLI toolchain that bundles three development utilities into a single installable package with zero third-party dependencies. It uses only the standard library, ensuring cross-platform compatibility.

The suite includes: rtree, which generates plain-text ASCII directory trees with configurable depth and automatic .gitignore-aware pruning; locr, which counts lines of code per file with language identification, percentage breakdowns, and JSON output; and gitmig, which copies repository directories while stripping .git history, node_modules, and virtual environments, with support for dry-run previews and ZIP archival.
features: locr: Lines of Code Counting with Language Breakdown
gitmig: Repository Copy with Bloat Stripping
installation:
# Clone and install
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/gtrmrs.git
cd gtrmrs
pip install -e .

# Usage
rtree
locr
gitmig ./backup --dry-run

title: Plant Disease Detector
description: A Django web application that uses TensorFlow and a fine-tuned MobileNetV2 model to classify apple leaf diseases from uploaded images. Renders prediction confidence via Plotly.js charts and persists scan history in SQLite.
longDescription:
This application demonstrates deploying a deep learning image classification model within a Django web framework. A MobileNetV2 convolutional neural network, pre-trained on ImageNet and fine-tuned via transfer learning on the PlantVillage dataset, classifies apple leaf images into four categories: Apple Scab, Black Rot, Cedar Apple Rust, and Healthy.

The interface provides a drag-and-drop image upload zone built with Bootstrap. Uploaded images are processed through the TensorFlow inference pipeline, and prediction probabilities are rendered as interactive charts via Plotly.js. All scans are automatically saved to a local SQLite database for historical reference. The model is scoped to apple leaves only and is designed as an educational proof of concept.
features: TensorFlow Inference for 4 Apple Leaf Classes
SQLite Scan History with Past Results
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/plant-disease-detector.git
cd plant-disease-detector

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Setup environment
cp .env.example .env

# Run migrations and start server
uv run python manage.py migrate
uv run python manage.py runserver

title: PC Remote Control
description: A Python FastAPI server that turns any mobile browser into a remote control for a desktop workstation. Supports media playback, volume control, system power operations, remote screenshots, and browser launching all over the local network.
longDescription:
PC Remote Control runs a lightweight FastAPI server on the desktop that serves a vanilla HTML/JS mobile interface accessible from any device on the same Wi-Fi network. It uses PyAutoGUI for simulating keyboard inputs and MSS for screen capture.

The mobile interface provides media playback controls (play/pause, volume, track navigation), system power operations (lock, sleep, reboot, shutdown), and a remote screenshot feature that captures the desktop screen and serves it as a compressed JPEG to the mobile display. A browser launcher button can open the default web browser on the PC remotely. The application runs with minimal resource overhead (~40-60MB RAM).
features: Media Playback & Volume Control via PyAutoGUI
System Power Operations (Sleep, Lock, Reboot, Shutdown)
Remote Desktop Screenshot Capture via MSS
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/pc-rc.git
cd pc-rc

# Install dependencies and run
uv sync
uv run main.py

title: E-commerce Website
description: An archived Django e-commerce platform with hierarchical product catalogs, Razorpay payment integration, wishlist management, OTP email verification, and Bootstrap-based server-side rendering.
longDescription:
EShopper is a monolithic Django e-commerce application that manages the full purchasing lifecycle through server-side rendering. It routes users from categorized product catalogs through a discount coupon system to a multi-stage checkout flow integrated with the Razorpay API.

The platform uses Django's session management for transient shopping carts and persistent wishlists. Account registration and password recovery are secured behind email-delivered OTP verification. The frontend is built with Django templates and Bootstrap for server-side rendered product listings, 5-star reviews, and multi-parameter search results. This project has been superseded by Amanzon.
features: Hierarchical Product Catalog with Category Navigation
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/eshopper
cd shopping

# Setup Python Environment
python -m venv venv
# Activate venv (Windows: venv\Scripts\activate, Linux: source venv/bin/activate)
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Initialize Database
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py createsuperuser

# Run Server
python manage.py runserver

title: Tremors
description: A multi-mode personal portfolio website built with Next.js. Features 5 distinct viewing modes (Cards, Terminal, AI Newspaper, Resume, Nexus Space), GitHub integration, AI-generated content via Google Gemini, and a fully functional interactive CLI.
longDescription:
Tremors is a personal portfolio application built on Next.js with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. It offers five distinct viewing modes: a modern card grid (Default), an interactive CLI terminal with 30+ commands (Terminal), an AI-generated newspaper mode (Newspaper), a professional resume view (Paper), and a 3D space landing (Nexus).

The Newspaper mode uses the Google Gemini Flash API with a "Skye" news agent that generates content in 4 distinct personalities (Tabloid, Senior, Scholar, Hacker) based on real GitHub activity. A PostgreSQL database managed by Prisma ORM stores project metadata, cached GitHub API data, and admin sessions. A hidden admin interface accessible through the Terminal mode provides CMS-style controls for managing project visibility, feature flags, and content editing with HMAC-signed authentication.
features: 5 Viewing Modes (Cards, Terminal, Newspaper, Resume, Nexus)
AI Content Generation via Google Gemini (4 Personalities)
GitHub Integration for Repository Stats & Activity
Hidden Admin CMS with HMAC-Signed Authentication
installation:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/tremors.git
cd tremors/app

# Setup Environment
npm install
cp .env.example .env.local
# Edit .env.local with your values

# Initialize Database
npx prisma generate
npx prisma db push

# Run Development Server
npm run dev

title: The Scrapyard
description: A cyberpunk-themed digital archive for abandoned and unfinished web projects. Built with a custom Node.js static site generator that scans project directories and compiles HTML pages with live preview iframes and canvas-based digital rain.
longDescription:
The Scrapyard is an interactive digital archive that showcases abandoned and scrapped web project prototypes in a cyberpunk-themed interface. A custom Node.js build script scans a projects directory, reads metadata, and compiles static HTML pages with template injection.

The interface features terminal-style search filtering, live preview iframes triggered on card hover, and a throttled HTML5 Canvas digital rain background animation. Projects are organized into categories (Portfolio, Games, 3D Models, Components, Demos) with color-coded visual indicators. The site is deployed via GitHub Actions to GitHub Pages.
features: HTML5 Canvas Digital Rain Animation (30fps Throttled)
Live Preview Iframes on Card Hover
Cyberpunk UI with Glitch Effects & Neon Accents
installation:
# Clone and build
git clone https://github.com/qtremors/scrap.git
cd scrap

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Build the site
npm run build

# Serve locally
npx serve
